verses.text: The scribes sit in Moses' seat; do as they teach, not as they do, for 'they say, and do not.' Hollow leaders. They lay heavy loads on others they will not bear themselves.
The scribes sit in Moses' seat; do as they teach, not as they do, for 'they say, and do not.' Hollow leaders. They lay heavy loads on others they will not bear themselves.
The scribes sit in Moses' seat; do as they teach, not as they do, for 'they say, and do not.' Hollow leaders. They lay heavy loads on others they will not bear themselves.
The scribes sit in Moses' seat; do as they teach, not as they do, for 'they say, and do not.' Hollow leaders. They lay heavy loads on others they will not bear themselves.
They do their works 'to be seen of men,' loving chief seats, greetings, and titles. Vanity. Their religion is a performance for applause.
They do their works 'to be seen of men,' loving chief seats, greetings, and titles. Vanity. Their religion is a performance for applause.
They do their works 'to be seen of men,' loving chief seats, greetings, and titles. Vanity. Their religion is a performance for applause.
Be not called Rabbi; 'he that is greatest among you shall be your servant'; the self-exalting shall be abased. True greatness. Humility, not rank, is honoured in the kingdom.

Be not called Rabbi; 'he that is greatest among you shall be your servant'; the self-exalting shall be abased. True greatness. Humility, not rank, is honoured in the kingdom.
Woe, ye hypocrites, who shut up the kingdom and make converts 'twofold more the child of hell.' Closed doors. They neither enter nor let others in.
Woe, ye hypocrites, who shut up the kingdom and make converts 'twofold more the child of hell.' Closed doors. They neither enter nor let others in.
Woe, ye hypocrites, who shut up the kingdom and make converts 'twofold more the child of hell.' Closed doors. They neither enter nor let others in.
Woe, blind guides, splitting hairs over what oaths bind; 'ye fools and blind.' Foolish casuistry. They twist sacred things to evade plain honesty.
Woe, blind guides, splitting hairs over what oaths bind; 'ye fools and blind.' Foolish casuistry. They twist sacred things to evade plain honesty.
Woe, blind guides, splitting hairs over what oaths bind; 'ye fools and blind.' Foolish casuistry. They twist sacred things to evade plain honesty.
Woe, blind guides, splitting hairs over what oaths bind; 'ye fools and blind.' Foolish casuistry. They twist sacred things to evade plain honesty.
He that swears by the altar swears by all on it, and by the temple, and by God who dwells there. No escape. Every oath ultimately invokes God Himself.
He that swears by the altar swears by all on it, and by the temple, and by God who dwells there. No escape. Every oath ultimately invokes God Himself.
He that swears by the altar swears by all on it, and by the temple, and by God who dwells there. No escape. Every oath ultimately invokes God Himself.
Ye tithe mint but omit 'judgment, mercy, and faith'; ye 'strain at a gnat, and swallow a camel.' Lost proportion. They fuss over trifles and neglect the weighty things.
Ye tithe mint but omit 'judgment, mercy, and faith'; ye 'strain at a gnat, and swallow a camel.' Lost proportion. They fuss over trifles and neglect the weighty things.
Ye cleanse the cup's outside but within are full of extortion; cleanse first the inside. Inward purity. God looks past the surface to the heart.
Ye cleanse the cup's outside but within are full of extortion; cleanse first the inside. Inward purity. God looks past the surface to the heart.
Ye are like whited sepulchres, fair without but full of dead men's bones. Beautiful decay. Outward show cannot hide inner corruption from God.
Ye are like whited sepulchres, fair without but full of dead men's bones. Beautiful decay. Outward show cannot hide inner corruption from God.
Ye build the prophets' tombs yet share your fathers' guilt; 'how can ye escape the damnation of hell?' Inherited sin. They honour dead prophets while persecuting living ones.

Ye build the prophets' tombs yet share your fathers' guilt; 'how can ye escape the damnation of hell?' Inherited sin. They honour dead prophets while persecuting living ones.
He sends prophets whom they kill; all the righteous blood shall come on this generation. Filled measure. The long account of bloodshed comes due at last.
He sends prophets whom they kill; all the righteous blood shall come on this generation. Filled measure. The long account of bloodshed comes due at last.
He sends prophets whom they kill; all the righteous blood shall come on this generation. Filled measure. The long account of bloodshed comes due at last.
'O Jerusalem... how often would I have gathered thy children... and ye would not!' Your house is left desolate. Yearning love. Christ weeps over the city that spurned His grace.
